hero.title: Joshua Hong
hero.metaLine: 2025–26 Freshman Season | UTSA Men's Golf | NCAA Division I
hero.definition: Record-level freshman scorer with verified Division I production, high counting reliability, and upward Power 4 lineup potential.
hero.supporting: Season-long profile built on 32 of 33 counting rounds, one win, three top-3 finishes, five top-10s, and 19 par-or-better scorecards across 33 official rounds.

quickSummary: Set UTSA’s single-season scoring average record at 71.73 over 33 official rounds.
College-level proof already includes an individual win, three top-3 finishes, and five top-10s.
Low-round capability is documented by a 64 (-8), a 134 (-10), and six sub-70 rounds.
Transfer read centers on lineup readiness, scoring stability, and room to grow into a higher team role.

metrics.label: Clippd Adjusted Avg
metrics.note: Division I scoring baseline with real lineup value.

metrics.label: Head-to-Head
metrics.note: 68.64% win rate across the full sample.

metrics.label: Top Finishes
metrics.note: Win, top-3, and top-10 volume across 11 freshman events.

stages.title: Junior-to-College Scoring Translation
stages.desc: The first college season showed that Joshua’s pre-college scoring pattern carried into NCAA team play through steady contribution, low-round capability, and record-level UTSA scoring.

stages.title: Back-to-Back Mexico Season Champion
stages.desc: Six consecutive wins and a second straight Mexico season title reinforced finishing ability, cumulative control, and long-form scoring value.

stages.title: Season Champion Before the Wins
stages.desc: The early profile separated through consistency, stability, and score accumulation before the later win volume arrived.

points: Individual champion and only player under par in the field.
Won at TPC San Antonio in a field where par held clear scoring value.
Contributed to UTSA’s team title in the same event.
Team-high Clippd weighted contribution: 403.10 points, 28.20% team share across 33 rounds.
Counting record: 32 of 33 team rounds as a freshman.
Round range: 64–76 across 33 official rounds, with no 80-plus scorecards.

evidenceProjection.projectedRoleBody: Lineup-ready transfer profile with verified freshman production, scoring stability, and room to grow into a higher team role.
evidenceProjection.translationText: Reliable scoring profile for programs seeking lineup competition, scoring stability, and upward role potential.
evidenceProjection.developmentText: Freshman-year production, scoring progression, and low-round evidence point to continued growth as his college schedule and role expand.

items.note: 28.20% Clippd points share across 33 rounds, with 403.10 weighted points.

items.label: Scoring Floor
items.note: Round range stayed within 64–76 across 33 official rounds, with no 80-plus scorecards.

cultureFit: Works within team structure and daily practice expectations.
Maintains steady preparation habits across tournament weeks.
Responsive to coaching feedback and technical adjustment.
Handled meaningful team scoring responsibility as a freshman.
Reliable teammate profile with consistent competitive preparation.

transferObjective.body: Evaluating a next-step program fit centered on continued player development, broader competitive exposure, season-to-season growth, and long-term college golf progression, while building on the competitive foundation, adaptation, and team experience gained during his freshman year at UTSA.
transferObjective.noteText: Grateful for the UTSA freshman-year experience and the opportunity to compete, adapt, learn, and contribute within the program; the next-step evaluation is focused on long-term development fit and continued growth.
